report_name: CISA Mandates Decommission of Medical IoT Gateways Due to 'Vitals Vapor' Zero-Day

attack_patterns.name: Manipulation of View
attack_patterns.description: The 'Vitals Vapor' exploit manipulates the data displayed to medical staff by freezing real-time patient vital signs and replaying pre-recorded loops of normal data, hiding the true state of the patient.

malware.name: Vitals Vapor
malware.description: A zero-day exploit that targets legacy embedded IoT gateways in medical facilities, allowing an attacker to manipulate patient monitoring data feeds by freezing live data and looping pre-recorded normal data.

vulnerabilities.name: Vitals Vapor
vulnerabilities.description: A zero-day vulnerability in legacy embedded IoT gateways used in medical facilities. It allows an attacker to compromise patient monitoring systems and manipulate the data feed to hide a patient's true condition.
affected_products: Legacy embedded IoT gateways in medical facilities

indicators.name: Insecure Protocol Usage on Medical Device Network Segment
indicators.pattern:
title: Insecure Protocol Usage on Medical Device Network Segment
id: 2b8a1c9d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects insecure protocols like Telnet or FTP, often found on legacy IoT/OT devices like the ones that could be vulnerable to exploits like 'Vitals Vapor'.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEgf1yUeXCo5kjDkE_6xDJTVTJ0a5oRApwTg86x3WlhyXTW6CYo4DPzvCvNCdXQOjT_I61h85fv2VU4L3ECv3aYGec9CNU8RscfWm9YVwiaxgMm2azL8sS8DiApoYoZfG-ytuNia6M=
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-05
tags:
  - attack.t1071.002
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationPort:
      - 21
      - 23
    Initiated: 'true'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of insecure protocols such as Telnet or FTP, which may indicate the presence of vulnerable legacy IoT devices as mentioned in the article.

relationships.source_name: Insecure Protocol Usage on Medical Device Network Segment
